front: What is the Rule of Sum (Addition Principle)?
back: If a task can be done in n ways or in m ways, and the two sets of ways are disjoint (no overlap), then the number of ways to do the task is n + m.

front: What is the Rule of Product (Multiplication Principle)?
back: If a task consists of a sequence of k steps, and step 1 can be done in n₁ ways, step 2 in n₂ ways, ..., and step k in nₖ ways, then the total number of ways to complete the task is n₁ × n₂ × ... × nₖ.

If k + 1 or more objects are placed into k boxes, then at least one box contains two or more objects. More generally, if n objects are placed into k boxes, then at least one box contains at least ⌈n/k⌉ objects.

front: What is the definition of a permutation?
back: An ordered arrangement of a set of distinct objects. The number of permutations of n objects taken r at a time is P(n, r) = n! / (n - r)!.

front: What is the definition of a combination?
back: An unordered selection of a subset of distinct objects from a set. The number of combinations of n objects taken r at a time is C(n, r) = n! / (r!(n - r)!).
